reason: Org-specific legal/consent facts an agent lacks; order traces to real external mandate.
Mechanical, regex-checkable SQL rules left as prose review instead of a linter script.
Concrete non-default style facts (sentence case, split threshold); low-stakes, no false guarantee.
Explicitly prices portability-vs-enforcement trade-off; checklist honest about what prose form can verify.
Org-specific webhook facts (retry schedule, dedupe, event types) an agent can't infer alone.
Highest-stakes irreversible-deletion gate leaves count/checksum verification as prose, not a compiled checker.
Generic debugging steps any capable agent already follows; fake mandatory ordering adds nothing.
Three reviewers, screenshots, 24h cooling imposed even on trivial typo fixes.
Domain-specific semver tightenings compiled into check_bump.py verifier; non-skippable exit done right.
Script only checks headers/word-count/keywords, falsely certifying 'analytical soundness' it can't judge.
Org-specific handoff format with concrete grep-checkable exit; genuine gap, no false claims.
No verifiable exit; 'quality' section is vague adjectives (inspiring, feel right), unfalsifiable.
